answer: The London metropolitan population in 2024 was approximately 9.7 million. This figure is based on projections from the Office for National Statistics. The population has continued to grow since then.

results.title: London, UK Metro Area Population (1950-2026) - Macrotrends
results.snippet: ## London, UK Metro Area Population (1950-2026). ##### The current metro area population of London in 2026 is **9,927,000**, a **0.87% increase** from 2025. * The metro area population of London in 2025 was **9,841,000**, a **0.95% increase** from 2024. * The metro area population of London in 2024 was **9,748,000**, a **1.04% increase** from 2023. * The metro area population of London in 2023 was **9,648,000**, a **1.12% increase** from 2022. The full historical dataset is available for download here: London, UK Metro Population | Historical Data | 1950-2026. ## Total Population.

results.title: London becomes a Megacity with population to hit nearly 10m by 2024 | Daily Mail Online
results.snippet: Most of the growth by 2024 will be in the South and East. An interactive guide allows you to find out what your area will look like in 2024 as projections from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) lays bare the impact of Britain's open borders policy in every borough across England. In October, the ONS released projections showing that nearly 10million more people will be living in Britain as a whole in 25 years, with immigration the overwhelming reason for the increase. The ONS said the population of England will go up by 7.5 per cent – or 4.1million people – between 2014 and 2024. The population of the East of England will go up by 8.9 per cent and the South East by 8.1 per cent over the same time. London is by far due to grow the fastest, by around 13.7 per cent on the 8.5 million figure from mid-2014, whereas the north east is projected to grow at the slowest rate over the next eight years.

results.title: Population of London predicted to near 10 million in a decade
results.snippet: # London population predicted to near 10 million within a decade as England continues to grow. The population of London is expected to near 10 million people within a decade as the capital remains the fastest-growing region in England. Suzie Dunsmith, from the ONS’ population projections unit, said: “All regions of England are projected to see an increase in their population size over the next decade, with London, the East of England and South East projected to grow faster than the country as a whole. All regions of the country are expected to see their populations but the slowest increase expected by the ONS is in the North East, on 3.1 per cent, followed by the North West and Yorkshire and the Humber. The number of local authorities where more than a quarter of residents are aged 65 and over is projected to increase from 28 out of 326 in mid-2014 to 84 areas in mid-2024 as the population continues to age.
